entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into a form usable by plants

entities.name: GRAIN LEGUMES
entities.description: Leguminous plants grown for their seeds, capable of nitrogen fixation

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low and erratic rainfall and specific environmental challenges

entities.name: SOIL NITROGEN
entities.description: The pool of nitrogen available in the soil for plant growth

entities.name: CEREAL CROPS
entities.description: Grass-based crops grown for their edible grains

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The measure of agricultural production per unit area

entities.name: NUTRIENT AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The accessibility of essential nutrients for plant uptake in the soil

entities.name: SOIL STRUCTURE
entities.description: The arrangement of soil particles into aggregates

entities.name: PESTS AND DISEASES
entities.description: Biological stressors that negatively impact crop health and productivity

entities.name: HORMONAL EFFECTS
entities.description: The influence of plant hormones on growth and development

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agricultural practices that maintain productivity while preserving environmental health

entities.name: BACTERIA
entities.description: Microorganisms that form symbiotic relationships with legumes to fix nitrogen

entities.name: MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Human-led agricultural interventions to optimize crop and soil health

entities.name: INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of adding specific bacteria to seeds or soil to enhance nitrogen fixation

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The physical, nutritional, and biological surroundings affecting plant growth
